name: Instagram MCP Server
what_it_does: Instagram MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Instagram — reading posts and media, fetching profile information, retrieving comments and engagement data, and integrating Instagram's social platform into agent-driven social media analytics and content management workflows.
use_cases: Fetching Instagram post metrics and engagement from analytics agents
Reading profile information and follower data from audience analysis agents
Retrieving recent posts and media content from monitoring agents
Analyzing Instagram content performance from marketing agents
Collecting hashtag and content data from research agents
Building social media reports from BI agents
not_for: Automated posting at scale (Instagram's API heavily restricts publishing)
Scraping without Meta developer app approval
Teams using TikTok, Twitter/X, or other platforms (use those MCPs)
best_when: An agent needs to read Instagram data for analytics — post metrics, profile information, or engagement data via the official Instagram Graph API.
avoid_when: You need to publish content at scale or access data without API approval — Instagram restricts automation heavily.

auth.notes: Meta/Facebook OAuth 2.0 via Instagram Graph API. Requires Meta developer app, Instagram Business or Creator account, and Facebook Page linkage. Complex multi-step setup. Access tokens expire and need refresh.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Instagram Graph API: free with Meta developer account (rate limited)
pricing.paid_tiers: Instagram Marketing API requires Meta Business Partner approval
pricing.notes: Instagram Graph API is free but requires Meta developer app and Business account. Advanced publishing APIs require Meta partner status. Community MCP is free.

performance.rate_limits: Instagram API: 200 calls/hour per user token; app-level limits vary

known_agent_gotchas: Instagram Graph API requires Business/Creator account linked to Facebook Page — personal accounts not supported
OAuth setup requires Meta Developer App, review process, and Instagram account linkage — days to set up
Access tokens expire (short-lived) — must implement token refresh for long-running agents
Publishing to Instagram requires additional permissions review by Meta
Community MCP — Instagram API changes frequently; server may break without warning
Rate limits are strict: 200 calls/hour per user token; aggressive use gets blocked
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with Meta. GDPR. Community MCP. Store access/refresh tokens securely. Instagram App permissions are granular — request minimum required scopes. Token refresh flow must be implemented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. OAuth 2.0 with Meta. GDPR. Community MCP. Granular scopes — request minimum. Secure token storage required.
